content.text: When user selects a Component Dent test (e.g., L1 or L2) from the Rules and Action test names list, system shall open a right-side configuration drawer Component Dent (L2)- local
a configuration drawer to open when I select a Component Dent test
I can view and configure the test rule.
I am on the Rules and Action test names list,
I select a Component Dent test (e.g., L1 or L2),
the system opens a right-side configuration drawer.

2.title: Display Test Name and ID in Header

content.text: System shall display the test name and ID in the panel header (e.g., Component Splice (L3), Component Thickness (L4)).
see the test name and ID in the configuration panel header
I know which test rule I am editing.
the system shows the test name and ID (e.g., Component Dent (L2), Component Thickness (L4)).
System shall display a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test (e.g., Surface depression severity evaluated using wall thickness ratio.)
a descriptive subtitle explaining the test purpose
I understand what the test evaluates.
the system displays a descriptive subtitle explaining the purpose of the test.
System shall display the test scope badge (e.g., Local) in the header.
see the test scope badge in the header
I know whether the rule is Global or Local.
the system shows the test scope badge (e.g., Local).
System shall display Measured Parameters section listing Dent Depth (d) and Nominal Wall Thickness (t).
see the measured parameters for the test
I understand the inputs used for the test name.
the system lists Dent Depth (d) and Nominal Wall Thickness (t).
System shall display text that “Thickness is automatically derived from Section library, OEM design, or field reference measurement.
an explanation of how thickness is derived
the Measured Parameters section is visible,
the system displays the text explaining that thickness is automatically derived from Section library, OEM design, or field reference measurement.
System shall automatically derive Thickness (t) from Section Library, OEM design data, or field reference measurement.
the system to automatically derive thickness
I do not need to manually input it.
the system derives Thickness (t) from the Section Library, OEM design data, or field reference measurement.
System shall display Severity Logic section with color-coded conditions (Green, Amber, Red).
I understand how results are classified.
the system displays a Severity Logic section with Green, Amber, and Red conditions.
System shall define Green condition as d ≤ 1.0 × t.
I know when a measurement is acceptable.
the Severity Logic section is displayed,
it is defined as d ≤ 1.0 × t.
System shall define Amber condition as 1.0 × t < d < 2.0 × t.
I know when a measurement requires attention.
the Severity Logic section is displayed,
it is defined as 1.0 × t < d < 2.0 × t.
System shall define Red condition as d ≥ 2.0 × t.
the Red severity condition clearly defined
I understand when the dent severity becomes critical.
the Component Dent evaluation model is displayed,
the Severity Logic section is visible,
the system defines the Red condition as d ≥ 2.0 × t.

12.title: Display Visual Indicators for Severity Levels

content.text: System shall display visual indicators for each severity level (green, amber, red).
visual indicators for each severity level
I can quickly interpret severity classifications.
the Severity Logic section is displayed,
the system displays visual indicators for Green, Amber, and Red severity levels.

13.title: Display Interaction with Bend Test Rule

content.text: System shall display Interaction with Bend Test rule: If both Dent and Bend exist, Final Severity = Worst of (Dent, Bend). Worst condition governs.
see the interaction rule between Dent and Bend tests
I understand how the final severity is determined when both defects exist.
the system shows the rule stating Final Severity = Worst of (Dent, Bend) and indicates that the worst condition governs.
System shall display “System Defined” label in footer for non-configurable tests.
see a “System Defined” label in the footer
I understand the configuration is predefined and cannot be modified.
the system displays the label “System Defined” indicating the test configuration is non-configurable.
System shall ensure thresholds and formulas displayed are non-editable for system-defined tests.
thresholds and formulas locked for system-defined tests
I cannot accidentally modify predefined engineering rules.
the test is marked as system-defined,
the thresholds and formulas are displayed,
the system ensures these values are non-editable.
System shall provide Close button to exit configuration drawer.
a Close button in the configuration drawer
I can exit the panel after reviewing the test configuration.
the system provides a Close button.
System shall display a close (X) icon in the header to dismiss the panel.
a close (X) icon in the panel header
I can quickly dismiss the configuration drawer.
the system shows a close (X) icon in the header.

18.title: Return to Testing List After Closing Panel

content.text: When user clicks Close or the close icon, system shall dismiss the side panel and return to the Rules and Action list – Testing tab.
the system to return me to the Rules and Action Testing tab after closing the panel
I can continue reviewing other test configurations.
I click the Close button or the close (X) icon,
the system dismisses the side panel and returns to the Rules and Action list – Testing tab.
